decisions.title: Runner home: wasc-local driver vs new monorepo sibling package
decisions.context: Runner home: wasc-local driver in prompt-workshop/dispatch/ (file:// deep imports) vs a new monorepo sibling package pi-workflows-cli. Both constraint-compliant (no pi-workflows change); pick before runner impl.
decisions.decision: Wasc-local driver in prompt-workshop/dispatch/ using absolute file:// deep imports of the pi-workflows dist/ engine functions. Sibling package rejected: it adds a package in another repo for no functional gain (only createAgentLoader gains a bare import) and is ecosystem scope the task does not need; the workshop tooling and substrate live in the wasc repo and absolute file:// is the established ESM-loader pattern here.
consequences: Determines where FEAT-001 runner code lives and how the engine is imported.

decisions.title: Runner ctx.cwd value: project root vs substrate root
decisions.context: Runner ctx.cwd value: the project root vs the substrate root — fix per where the run blocks/paths resolve.
decisions.decision: ctx.cwd is the wasc project root. The workflow .workflows discovery and run-dirs/artifacts belong in the project, not a substrate dir; the workflow uses no native block steps; command steps handle their own Django cd, uv, and DATABASE_URL.
consequences: Determines path/block resolution semantics for every workflow step.

decisions.title: Runner v1 agent-step scope: agent steps in v1 vs command/transform/gate-only first
decisions.context: Runner v1 agent-step scope: support agent steps in v1 (requires the pi-on-PATH + model-backend pre-flight) vs ship command/transform/gate-only first and add agent support later.
decisions.decision: Agent steps in v1. The workflow call leg is an agent step (14 across the sequence); a command/transform/gate-only runner cannot run the workshop. TASK-007 (pi-on-PATH plus model-backend pre-flight) is in scope. Agent dispatch is runtime-verified (A6).
consequences: Gates whether the agent-step pre-flight task is in v1 scope (the conditional task).

decisions.title: C2 flag-surfacing reuses the existing client nav, result-frame catalogue, and the C1 Flag model — no A3 or C1 extension
decisions.context: TASK-020 / FGAP-008 (surface A3 flags to the client, mark the specific element each concerns, and offer accept / replace / request-catalogue-addition) raised three apparent forks: how a flag addresses its DOM element, how the client offers a catalogued replacement, and where a resolution persists. Each is settled by current source, not a user choice.
decisions.decision: Addressing: the client maps a flag to its row by reusing the existing prefill->row navigation (targetFormset / directRows / nestedFormset / stepChildFormset), parsing the flag's spec-prose `field` by the same index structure the apply functions already use — no `element_path` on FreetextFlag, no parser / A3 change. Catalogue: `build_catalogue_union(grounding)` rides the assist result frame (there is no client catalogue endpoint). Resolution maps onto the existing C1 Flag model: accept keeps the value (no row); replace substitutes the flagged span in the draft field (no row); request-catalogue-addition persists one Flag row (is_resolved=False) created in PlanCreateView.post after the plan PK exists — no `resolution_action` field, no C1 extension.
consequences: C2 touches only planner/views.py (result frame + create-view persistence), planner/static/wizard/js/ai-assist.js, and tests; FreetextFlag / the parsers (A3) and the Flag model / a migration (C1) are unchanged.
Request-catalogue-addition rows (is_resolved=False) are the durable admin signal; accept and replace are corrected in the draft content and leave no row.
Flag persistence is bound to plan creation, the only point a Plan PK exists; assist-time resolutions are client form-state until the create POST carries them.

references.label: result frame drops flags (criterion-1 change site)
client prefill->row navigation reused for flag addressing
C1 Flag model — the persistence target
plan PK created at the create POST

decisions.title: Human-decision-surface placement: options-posture at the orchestration/UX layer; unowned-precursor decision-requests as in-draft plan content
decisions.context: C3/R8 (options-posture) and FGAP-040 (upward decision-requests for unowned precursors) both ask WHERE human-decides surfaces live, given per-spec generation stays decide-one (the preamble's no-options-instead-of-decisions rule). Originally framed as an open decision; on inspection the placement is DERIVABLE from the app's stated purpose, not a free choice. Stated purpose: DEC-29 north-star + the US-DRAFT frame (00-preamble) -- the AI renders a COMPLETE deliverable draft from full DB context, the school admin then fine-tunes it via the create-plan UX (versioned saves), and the admin alone flips draft->active. DEC-41 consequence 4 + its UX clause -- the orchestration trio US-LLM-23/24/25 is the production realization, and its UX surfaces decide-points to admins as actionable items. The plan is also a real deliverable document (TASK-037 .docx, Sample Schoolwide Action Plan shape) whose content includes the decisions/dependencies a real action plan records.
decisions.decision: Two human-decision kinds, two placements, each DERIVED from the stated purpose (not selected among alternatives). (1) OPTIONS-POSTURE (R8) -- genuine value/scope forks a leader must set (e.g. the target increment) -> resolved at the ORCHESTRATION / create-plan-UX layer, realized with the US-LLM-23/24/25 trio. Derivation: a leader setting a value IS 'the human fine-tunes the rendered draft', which US-DRAFT + DEC-41 locate at the UX/orchestration surface; per-spec generation stays decide-one (commits a best default), preserving the no-options-instead-of-decisions rule. (2) DECISION-REQUESTS (FGAP-040) -- an action-step precursor no division owns -> authored as IN-DRAFT plan content, the SAME account-for-every-item-or-flag-it class as coverage (FGAP-037/041 pattern: assign an owner, OR record an explicit unowned decision-request with a reason). Derivation: an unowned precursor is a planning finding the COMPLETE draft must account for, and a deliverable action plan carries decisions/dependencies-to-resolve as body content; buildable now on the coverage pattern, independent of the trio. No corpus body changed by this record (decision only).
consequences: FGAP-040 placement is settled: an in-draft authored discipline on the FGAP-037/041 coverage pattern (snippet contract + parser-recognized element + per-spec in-execution self-check), buildable independent of the trio; its build is a future task the human scopes.
R8 options-posture remains an orchestration/UX concern realized when US-LLM-23/24/25 is built; no per-spec snippet emits option menus.
Per-spec generation invariant (decide-one) reaffirmed: snippets commit a best default and never emit options-instead-of-decisions.
Placement decision only -- zero changes to any snippet, preamble, or parser body attributable to this record (corpus diff = zero).

references.label: DEC-29 north-star: render complete draft -> admin fine-tunes/flips; US-LLM-23/24/25 orchestration
US-DRAFT frame: renders draft -> user fine-tunes; versioned saves
DEC-41 consequence 4 + UX clause: trio = production realization; UX surfaces decide-points to admins
FGAP-037/041 coverage pattern (account-for-every-item-or-flag-it) reused by FGAP-040
